preamble: The shift from the voiced alveolar stop [d] to the voiceless palato-alveolar affricate [tʃ] involves three steps: palatalization (due to a following [i] or [j]), affrication, and finally devoicing. It is often seen in rapid speech or as a historical progression of 'yod-coalescence'.

examples.note: Yod-coalescence first produces the voiced affricate [dʒ], which can devoice to [tʃ] in rapid or informal speech.
